big_picture: The CPI(ML) Chief criticized Nitish Kumar, calling it a "Betrayal Of People's Mandate." Prime Minister Modi stated that "No Issue Can Be Resolved Through Military Conflict." Rahul Gandhi claimed that "Conflict Reaches India’s Backyard" and accused the PM of silence. The Allahabad High Court's "Failure To Pronounce Verdict 6 Years After Reserving Forces" led the Supreme Court to transfer cases to itself. The Supreme Court issued notice to the Gujarat High Court and Gujarat Government on a plea challenging the "Gujarat Civil Judge Recruitment Process." The Kerala High Court will decide whether the state can send "Thank You" messages to donors of the Chief Minister's Disaster Relief Fund.

articles.title: GST Appeal Cannot Be Rejected Later for Manual Filing After Being Accepted & Heard on Merits
articles.summary: The High Court quashed the appellate order rejecting a GST appeal on the ground of manual filing. The Court held that once the authority accepted the appeal and heard it on merits, it could not dismiss it later on a technical ground. The post GST Appeal Cannot Be Rejected Later for Manual Filing After Being Accepted & Heard on Merits appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Assessment Void Because Notice Issued to Non-Existing Company After Merger: Bombay HC
articles.summary: The High Court held that a tax notice and assessment issued in the name of a company that had already merged into another entity were invalid. The ruling clarifies that once the tax authority is informed of a merger, proceedings must be issued in the name of the transferee company. articles.title: Employees' Compensation Act Claim Cannot Be Rejected Merely Because Disability Certificate Was Issued By Non-Treating Doctor: Bombay HC
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has held that an application for compensation under the Employees' Compensation Act, 1923, cannot be rejected solely on the ground that the disability certificate was issued by a doctor who did not attend to the injured workman. The Court observed that the Act only requires the certificate to be issued by a “qualified medical practitioner” and does not mandate that such a practitioner must have treated the injured. articles.title: Karnataka Revises Power Tariffs After Allowing DISCOMs’ Review Petition
articles.summary: The Karnataka Electricity Regulatory Commission (KERC) has revised power tariffs for agricultural, commercial, and industrial consumers in Karnataka. This change affects the financial year 2025-26. The revision was made after considering a review petition filed by the state's electricity distribution companies (DISCOMs).

articles.title: IEX Power Trade Surges 30% YoY to 12,550 MU in February
articles.summary: The Indian Energy Exchange (IEX) recorded a total electricity-traded volume of 12,550 million units (MU) in February 2026, a 30.4% year-over-year (YoY) increase, and its highest daily average volume of 448 MU. During the month, India’s electricity consumption reached 133 BU, up 1.9% YoY. Despite the rise in demand, electricity prices on the exchange declined due to increased liquidity in the supply.

articles.title: Middle East conflict poses near-term challenges to Indian economy: RBI MPC member
articles.summary: Middle East conflict presents short-term challenges for India's economy, including oil price hikes and export disruptions, but is unlikely to derail long-term growth. An RBI panel member highlighted the need for coordinated fiscal and monetary policies to achieve an 8% GDP growth trajectory, supported by manufacturing and services. Inflation remains benign, offering a 'Goldilocks' scenario.

Inflation remains benign, offering a 'Goldilocks' scenario.

articles.title: Police Should Record Statements, Preserve Videos Of Amicable Settlements In Criminal Cases: Orissa High Court
articles.summary: The Orissa High Court has recently observed that the police should record statements and preserve videos of amicable settlements between the parties in criminal cases, in order to ensure that the settlement is arrived at voluntarily at the consent of the parties, bereft of any pressure or threats. articles.title: 'Divorce Decree Cannot Be Granted By Relying Merely On Whatsapp Chat': Bombay High Court
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has held that a decree of divorce cannot be granted merely on the basis of WhatsApp chats without proper proof through evidence. The Court observed that allegations of cruelty under Section 13(1)(i-a) of the Hindu Marriage Act, 1955 must be established through legally admissible evidence, and the opposing party must be given an opportunity to rebut the material relied upon. A division bench of Justices Bharati Dangre and Manjusha Deshpande was hearing a Family Court Appeal

The Court further clarified that once a RERA order granting monetary relief attained finality, authorities were bound to enforce it through the statutory recovery mechanisms available under revenue law. Justice Jyotsna Rewal Dua

articles.title: Enforcement, yes — but with accountability: Understanding arrest provisions in GST law
articles.summary: The article explains the arrest provisions in India's GST law, emphasizing that while tax evasion cases justify such measures, arrests should be made with accountability. Former CBIC chairman Najib Shah highlights that legal grounds alone do not mandate arrests, ensuring a balanced approach to enforcement.
articles.raw_summary: Given the large-scale detection of tax evasion cases, which have a multiplier effect on the economy and may even fund anti-national activities, arrest provisions are necessary in fiscal laws. However, the Board’s circular makes it clear that the mere existence of legal grounds for arrest does not automatically mandate an arrest, writes former CBIC chairman Najib Shah.

articles.title: India's balance of payments outlook faces risks from West Asia crisis
articles.summary: India's external sector outlook remains broadly manageable but faces rising risks from geopolitical developments in West Asia that could push up crude oil prices and widen the current account deficit (CAD), according to a recent research note by Yes Bank. The report noted that India recorded a Balance of Payments (BoP) deficit of USD 24.4 billion in Q3 FY26, wider than the USD 10.9 billion deficit in the previous quarter, largely due to capital account outflows.

articles.title: Chandigarh Consumer Commission Holds WTC Chandigarh Liable For Delay In Possession; Homebuyers Cannot Be Made To Wait Indefinitely
articles.summary: The Chandigarh Consumer Commission ruled that WTC Chandigarh Development Company Pvt. Ltd. was liable for delaying possession of a residential unit beyond the agreed deadline. The court stated that homebuyers cannot be made to wait indefinitely, reinforcing consumer rights in real estate transactions. This ruling strengthens legal protections for homebuyers against developer delays.
articles.raw_summary: The State Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission, Chandigarh , comprising Justice Raj Shekhar Attri (President) and Preetinder Singh (Member), has held M/s WTC Chandigarh Development Company Pvt. Ltd. liable for deficiency in service for failing to complete construction of the unit and hand over possession within the stipulated deadline. The Commission observed that a homebuyer cannot be compelled to wait indefinitely for possession and that such delay defeats the very purpose of the agreement.

articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Pune ruled that creating artificial goodwill in an intra-group merger is a colourable transaction, disallowing depreciation claims. articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) remanded a case back to the Assessing Officer because the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) ignored additional evidence submitted by the taxpayer. This decision reinforces the importance of considering all evidence in tax appeals, ensuring fair proceedings for taxpayers.

articles.title: Study finds counterintuitive rise in elephant deaths after organised monitoring
articles.summary: A study found that villages in Assam with active elephant monitoring squads recorded two to three times more elephant deaths over two decades. The monitoring squads were set up in 2004 to reduce human-elephant conflict, but the unexpected rise in elephant fatalities raises questions about the effectiveness of current policies and may lead to future policy changes.

articles.title: Indian shipments take costlier, longer route to Europe amid US-Iran tensions
articles.summary: Security concerns around the Strait of Hormuz and the Red Sea–Suez Canal corridor have prompted major carriers to suspend or restrict transits, extending transit times by 10–20 days and raising freight rates by 40–50% on key India–Europe routes, according to industry executives and insurers.

articles.title: Manufacturing activity surges at fastest pace in four months in February: PMI survey shows
articles.summary: India’s manufacturing activity expanded at its fastest pace in four months in February, powered by strong domestic demand even as export growth softened, S amp;P Global’s PMI survey showed.

articles.title: Middle East war unlikely to dent India’s remittance inflows significantly
articles.summary: The US is now the largest source of inward remittances, accounting for 27.7% of such flows into India as of FY24, per latest data from the RBI. The UAE, which was the largest source in FY17, is now the second largest, while nations like Saudi Arabia and Kuwait slipped further down.

articles.title: Govt amends BIS rules to quicken product certification, tighten compliance norms
articles.summary: The Indian government has revised the BIS rules to speed up product certification and tighten compliance norms. This change places more emphasis on documentation scrutiny and third-party laboratory testing. The move aims to support manufacturing expansion in the country.
articles.raw_summary: Instead of relying heavily on prior on-site assessment, the revised framework places greater emphasis on documentation scrutiny, third-party laboratory testing and post-market surveillance, to support manufacturing expansion.

articles.title: Centre, states collect GST revenue of  ₹1.61 trillion in February after refunds
articles.summary: The Indian government collected ₹1.61 trillion in GST revenue in February, following a rate cut announced in September last year. The government hopes this will boost demand and support higher tax collections. This is a significant government action in the finance sector.
articles.raw_summary: The government has maintained that GST rate cuts announced in September last year would boost demand for goods and services and, over time, support higher tax collections.

articles.title: Will oil prices touch $100? What will be economic impact of US-Israel attack on Iran? Expert weighs in
articles.summary: The US and Israel are considering an attack on Iran, which could lead to a rise in global oil prices. This could have a significant impact on India's economy, particularly if oil prices reach $100 per barrel.
articles.raw_summary: The US and Israel's attack on Iran may significantly impact global oil prices and inflation, depending on the duration of the conflict and Iranian retaliation. Economists suggest oil could reach $80 per barrel, potentially rising to $100 if supply disruptions occur.

articles.title: Service sector activity slightly eases in February; exports pick up: PMI
articles.summary: India's services sector saw a slight slowdown in February, with new business growth at a 13-month low and operating costs at a two-and-a-half-year high. Despite this, overall services activity remained robust, supported by strong domestic demand and international sales, leading to increased optimism for the coming year.

articles.summary: India's services sector growth slowed in February, with new business rising at its slowest pace in over a year due to intensifying competition. Despite this, international sales surged, and firms passed on rising costs, primarily from food, energy, and labor, to customers. Overall private sector growth accelerated, driven by a brighter outlook and increased hiring.

The government is closely monitoring the situation.

India is taking emergency trade support measures to ensure continuity of essential imports.

articles.title: NHAI awards 111 km-long Odisha Ring Road project to three concessionaires
articles.summary: The National Highways Authority of India has awarded a major project to build the Bhubaneswar Bypass. This 111 km, six-lane ring road will connect Rameshwar to Tangi. articles.title: Qatar declares force majeure, gas supplies to Indian industry cut amid Iranian strikes
articles.summary: India faces significant energy disruption as Qatar halts liquefied natural gas deliveries. An Iranian drone strike has forced Qatar to declare force majeure. This halt impacts up to 40 percent of India's gas supplies. Industries are seeing cuts, while CNG and piped gas networks are prioritized. Companies are seeking alternative supplies in a firming spot market.

articles.summary: India's economy is growing through investment and reforms, not just stimulus. Public spending on infrastructure is rising, boosting competitiveness. Faster dispute resolution is also key.

articles.title: Ecological lessons for urban planners
articles.summary: The Indian government is proceeding with the Char Dham road widening project in the Bhagirathi Eco-Sensitive Zone, despite recent ecological disasters like Chamoli and Silkyara. This decision, highlighted in an HT editorial, raises concerns about balancing infrastructure development with environmental protection. The project's continuation could have long-term ecological and policy implications for India's eco-sensitive zones.
articles.raw_summary: Despite Chamoli and Silkyara, the Centre is keen to continue the char dham road widening project in the Bhagirathi Eco-Sensitive Zone
